definition: Encode each text sample with a task-appropriate biomedical, clinical, or general sentence embedding model, then compute train-eval cosine similarity or a dataset-level median cosine distance. This audits whether NLP performance decays as semantic content, specialty, note type, or corpus style moves away from the training set.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_text_embeddings.csv --eval eval_text_embeddings.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix emb_

citations.title: Quantification of BERT Diagnosis Generalizability Across Medical Specialties Using Semantic Dataset Distance

evidence.summary: The paper finds clinical NLP generalizability varies across specialties and correlates with train-test semantic similarity.
Semantic similarity between partitions is measured with cosine distances between sentence embeddings.
Median cosine distance separates internal, partial, and external train-test relations and is associated with AUC.

limitations: Embedding similarity depends on encoder choice and may miss label-specific semantic shifts.
Text style, metadata leakage, note templates, and institution-specific phrasing can dominate content similarity.
A single median distance can hide multimodal train-test overlap.
quality_gates: Report encoder name, pooling strategy, preprocessing, and whether embeddings were trained on evaluation text.
Run curves by text domain metadata such as specialty, institution, note type, or time period when available.
Inspect nearest training texts for privacy leakage and shortcut artifacts.
